limitations: Computed over only 10 forums from a single, still-active review cycle -- not a stable venue-wide baseline yet.
claims.text: Across this sample, the paper-level mean initial rating is 4.33 (population stdev 1.34, n=10 forums).

limitations: Decisions are 100% unresolved in this sample (active review cycle) -- this describes reviewer ratings only, not accept/reject outcomes.
claims.text: Of 37 individual reviewer ratings, 8 fall in the low tercile, 17 in the medium tercile, and 12 in the high tercile; none of the 10 forums have a posted decision yet.

limitations: A single reviewer's assessment of a single paper (initial rating 8/10), not a corpus-wide pattern.
claims.text: This is a simple and novel idea: the model becomes active, inspects its current memory/state and accordingly constructs the context to operate on using pre-defined tools.

limitations: A single reviewer's assessment (initial rating 6/10), not a corpus-wide pattern.
claims.text: The findings are valuable, particularly that CoT can *increase* hallucinations on HSS tasks  and that standard RAG provides no consistent benefit, highlighting the unique challenges of this domain.

limitations: A single reviewer's assessment (initial rating 2/10), not a corpus-wide pattern.
claims.text: It is challenging to identify sufficient additional value that this paper contributes to the existing literature.

limitations: A single reviewer's assessment (initial rating 2/10), not a corpus-wide pattern.
claims.text: The comparison primarily focuses on outcome-based versus rubric-based rewards, but an important baseline, generative rewards, is missing.

limitations: Based on only 2 of 37 reviews in a 10-forum sample -- an interpretive pattern, not a validated corpus-wide finding.
claims.text: In this small sample, both low-rated reviews cited here criticize the paper for not sufficiently distinguishing itself from -- or comparing against -- existing work, suggesting reviewers weigh differentiation from prior work heavily even when a method is technically sound.

limitations: The review cycle is still active and no revised-score snapshot has been fetched yet; see statistics.json's initial_to_final_rating_change (count=0).
claims.text: No initial-to-final rating changes have been captured for this sample yet, so no claim about rebuttal effectiveness can be supported.

limitations: A drift claim requires at least two venue-years of comparable statistics; this corpus currently has one.
claims.text: This sample covers only ICLR 2026, so no year-over-year drift claim can be supported yet.
